name: Clarence Environment Centre
description: Grassroots environment centre active since 1990. Runs a volunteer-staffed shop in Grafton, ecological surveys of vulnerable species, and advocacy across the Clarence Valley. Works with council, landowners and community groups to protect local biodiversity.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (phone + email inferred from domain pattern - verify)

name: Clarence Valley Conservation Coalition
description: Grafton-based community group formed 1988 with members throughout the Clarence Valley. Engages on environmental, land-use and biodiversity issues affecting the region.
source: org blog + environmental movement directory 2026-04-22 (email inferred - verify)

name: Valley Watch Inc
description: Long-running conservation group focused on the Lower Clarence. Funded the Lower Clarence Biolinks Study and liaises with government, industry and community on resource management and nature conservation.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (email from published directory listings - verify)

name: FoodCare Grafton
location_detail: Shop L5 'The Link', Grafton Shopping World, 50 Prince St, Grafton
description: Low-cost food relief operating Wed-Fri 9am-1pm. A care group of Christian Outreach Centre Grafton helping people under financial stress stretch their food budget.

name: Yamba Farmers & Producers Market
description: Weekly Wednesday producer-only farmers market at Whiting Beach. Anchor point for Clarence Valley regenerative producers and a hub for the local food story.

name: Casino Community Garden
location_detail: Cnr Adam & Hartley Streets, South Casino
description: Community garden growing food locally and supporting local welfare agencies. A project of the Casino Neighbourhood Centre.

name: Casino Neighbourhood Centre
description: Richmond Valley's Casino-based neighbourhood centre. Auspices the Casino Community Garden and delivers community development, welfare and family support across the LGA.

name: Mid Richmond Neighbourhood Centre (Community Pantry)
location_detail: Shop 5/6 Oak St, Oaks Arcade, Evans Head
description: Neighbourhood centre serving Evans Head, Woodburn, Broadwater and Coraki. Runs a low-cost Community Pantry ($3/yr membership), Cook and Connect program, and broader community services.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Ballina Community Garden
location_detail: Saunders Oval, cnr Swift & Clarence Sts, Ballina
description: Established 2011 on a 3,900 sqm Crown Land site. Welcoming, inclusive space for growing fruit, vegetables and flowers, and sharing gardening knowledge and local food sustainability skills.

name: Lennox Community Gardens
location_detail: Ocean Breeze Reserve, Lennox Head, Ballina LGA
description: Founded 2015 by local volunteers. Occupies 3,000 sqm within Ocean Breeze Reserve with ~100 mainly exotic fruit trees plus vegetable plots. Working bees Thursdays 8.30-10.30am and Sundays 4-6pm.

name: Lennox Head Landcare
description: Lennox Head Landcare acknowledges the Traditional Custodianship of the Nyangbul peoples of the Bundjalung Nation. Maintains habitat gardens and bush regeneration work across the Lennox area.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (email inferred from domain pattern - verify)

name: Liberation Larder
location_detail: Byron Bay Community Centre, Fletcher St entrance
description: Volunteer-run registered charity rescuing food from becoming waste and preparing meals for people in need. Serves 600+ meals per week from the Byron Community Centre kitchen (Mon/Thu 7am-2pm).
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Amrita Pantry Byron Bay
description: Food pantry initiative of Amrita Australia and Amma Byron Bay. Works with local organisations to provide food and essential items to people in need across Byron Bay and surrounds.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Santos Organics
location_detail: Three stores across Byron Bay + Mullumbimby
description: 100% not-for-profit environmental social enterprise pioneering organic and bulk food in the region since 1978. Became a registered environmental charity in 2016. Supports hundreds of local and organic farmers, employs 75+ locals across three stores & cafes.

name: Mullumbimby & District Neighbourhood Centre
description: Heart-of-the-community neighbourhood centre offering emergency relief, food parcels, frozen meals, free counselling, free community lunches Tue-Thu 12.30-1.30pm, and Wednesday soup kitchen. Gulganii Pantry runs low-cost food Mon/Wed/Fri.

name: Pottsville Beach Neighbourhood Centre
description: Tweed Coast neighbourhood centre offering community services to children, young people, families and seniors. Runs social enterprises including an op shop, tech centre, and community gardens.

name: New Brighton Farmers Market
location_detail: Tom Kendall Oval, River St, New Brighton, Byron LGA

name: Young Farmers Connect (Northern Rivers)
description: Not-for-profit uniting new, young and aspiring farmers. Northern Rivers is YFC's founding chapter, hosting bi-monthly field days, farm walks and socials focused on small-scale regenerative ecological farming.

name: The Grass Roots Mentoring Group
description: Formed 2018, grown to 80+ members focused on biodiversity and native habitat regeneration on private land. Meets on members' properties for peer mentoring, hands-on work, and information sharing.

name: Bangalow Land and Rivercare
description: Formed 1998 to improve water quality in Byron Creek through native revegetation. Weekly working bees Saturdays 8.30-10.30am across varying sites in the Bangalow area.

name: Wilsons Creek / Huonbrook Landcare Group
location_detail: Wilsons Creek, Huonbrook & Wanganui valleys, Byron LGA

name: Friends of the Koala
description: Koala hospital, care centre and Burribi Community Education Centre in Lismore. Runs a native-plant nursery supplying koala-habitat species and delivers community engagement & advocacy. 24-hour rescue hotline: 6622 1233.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (admin email inferred from domain pattern - verify)

name: Bangalow Koalas
description: Grass-roots not-for-profit that has planted 500,000+ trees across seven Northern Rivers shires since 2019, aiming for 850,000 by 2030. Works with councils, Landcare groups, landholders and communities to rebuild koala habitat corridors.
source: org website 2026-04-22

name: Jagun Alliance Aboriginal Corporation
description: Aboriginal-led organisation specialising in natural resource management and resilience for Country on Bundjalung land. Runs cultural landscape restoration, nature-based flood mitigation, cultural burning, water-quality monitoring and knowledge-sharing programs.
source: org website 2026-04-22 (email inferred from domain pattern; recommend direct outreach before publishing)

name: Fletcher Street Cottage
description: Homelessness service hub opened April 2022 by Byron Community Centre and Creative Capital. Provides breakfasts, showers, laundry and partner services (housing, medical, counselling, legal, vet, podiatry) Tue-Fri.

name: Kyogle Family Support Services Neighbourhood Centre
